full_text:
Single-cell spatial mapping unveils tumor-intrinsic MHC-II 
as a key driver of the spatial tumor ecosystem and clinical outcomes 
in high-grade serous ovarian cancer

Fernando Perez-Villatoro
Aleksandra Shabanova

Research Program in Systems Oncology, University of Helsinki, Finland

Doctoral student

‹#›

Outline

Summary of High grade serous ovarian cancer

Our study’s approach/methodology

Results - MHCII in cancer cells

‹#›

Outline

Summary of High grade serous ovarian cancer

Our study’s approach/methodology

Results - MHCII in cancer cells

‹#›

Detection at advanced stage

High grade serous ovarian cancers (HGSC) patients have a poor clinical prognosis

TCGA; 
Tumor stage
at diagnosis

Lack of treatment options

weillcornell.org

The standard of care is: Surgery + platinum chemotherapy (or PARP inhibitors)

5-year survival rate is less than 40%

‹#›

HGSC is characterized by common molecular alterations clinically relevant

Around 20% of samples have a BRCA1/2 alteration conferring a DNA-repair Homologous  recombination deficiency (HRD) 

Around 30% of samples have HRD alterations beyond BRCA1/2

Good responders to PARPi. 
Immune-inflamed tumors.

All these tumors have a TP53 mutation/alteration and harbour genomic lesions.

Poor responders to PARPi. 
Low (cold) immuned tumors.

CCNE1amp amplification in ~15%

The rest are HR proficient (HRP)

‹#›

Despite the high immune infiltration in HGSC, the response to 
immune checkpoint inhibitors has been very modest

E. Kandalaft, et al, 2022

‹#›

Outline

Summary of High grade serous ovarian cancer

Our study’s approach/methodology

Results - MHCII in cancer cells

‹#›

Multimodal HGSC study of the TME

‹#›

Cell type
annotation

Using image analysis we identified 15 immune populations, fibroblast, endothelial and cancer cells

‹#›

‹#›

MHC-II was the most differential marker to distinguish long or short OS among all critical features

‹#›

MHCI MHCII

Visual inspection of images confirms a cancer-cell intrinsic expression of MHC-II, and not always correlated with MHC-I

‹#›

Statistical validation confirms association of cancer-intrinsic MHCII with longer OS

‹#›

Validation confirms association of MHCII expression with response to platinum-based chemotherapy

‹#›

MHCII+ cancer cells aggregate
 in spatial celular neighborhoods

‹#›

MHCII+ cancer cells are often
located in immune hotspots

‹#›

Validation on independent cohort: MHCII+ cancer cells are often located in immune hotspots

‹#›

MHCII expression in cancer cells are significantly enriched with T cells and M1 macrophages

‹#›

Multimodal cohort

MCHII was associated with significantly upregulated genes related to immune activation, and INFg

‹#›

MHC-II expression in cancer cells 
linked with CD4.T cells and INFg in other studies

Margaret L. Axelrod, Clin Can Res, 2019

MHC-II expressed by APC cells

MHC-II expressed by cancer cells

‹#›

There in an increase in INF signal in dendritic cells and T.cells next to MHCII+ cancer cells

‹#›

There is a spatial correlation of INF-g with MHCII+ expression in tumoral areas

‹#›

Summary
